text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Raymond Kowalski
MRN: [REDACTED]
Accession Number: SP-[REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: Medical Oncology
Performing Physician/Radiologist: Interventional Radiology

---

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
Right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy

CLINICAL HISTORY:
58-year-old male [age at biopsy approximately 59 years] with known Stage IV (M1b) sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma, KRAS G12D mutant, pMMR/MSS. Patient initially presented with synchronous liver metastases and has been on ongoing systemic chemotherapy (FOLFOX plus bevacizumab x 12 cycles, followed by maintenance 5-FU/bevacizumab, subsequently switched to FOLFIRI plus ramucirumab at 10 months due to disease progression). Current imaging demonstrates a right lower lobe pulmonary nodule measuring 0.8 cm, present since progression scan at 10 months and stable on most recent surveillance imaging. Biopsy requested to histologically confirm metastatic disease and obtain tissue for molecular re-profiling. Clinical staging context: lung nodule has been present for approximately 7-8 months; additional bilateral pulmonary nodules identified at time of hepatic progression.

---

PROCEDURE:
CT-guided percutaneous core needle biopsy, right lower lobe pulmonary nodule. Coaxial technique, 20-gauge core biopsy needle, 3 passes. Real-time CT fluoroscopic guidance. Local anesthesia administered without complication. Specimens submitted in formalin for routine processing. Post-procedure CT confirmed no pneumothorax. Specimen adequacy confirmed on touch imprint by cytopathologist at time of procedure.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received in formalin, labeled "right lower lobe lung biopsy," three tan-white cylindrical tissue cores ranging from 0.8 to 1.2 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. Aggregate length: approximately 2.9 cm. Tissue cores are firm with focal areas of pallor and irregular texture. All material submitted entirely for histologic processing in one cassette.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections of the three core biopsy fragments show pulmonary parenchyma partially replaced by a moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma. The neoplastic glands are arranged in irregular tubular and cribriform configurations, set within a desmoplastic stroma. Tumor cells display moderate nuclear enlargement with vesicular chromatin, conspicuous nucleoli, and moderate pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Mitotic figures are present, approximately 4 per 10 high-power fields. Intraluminal necrotic debris ("dirty necrosis") is focally identified, a characteristic feature of colorectal-type adenocarcinoma. No mucinous or signet ring cell differentiation is identified. No squamous or neuroendocrine features are present.

The background pulmonary parenchyma shows focal alveolar collapse adjacent to the tumor mass and mild chronic inflammation. No granulomatous inflammation. No evidence of a primary lung-type adenocarcinoma pattern (no lepidic, acinar-predominant, papillary, or micropapillary architecture characteristic of primary lung adenocarcinoma in the surrounding parenchyma). Visceral pleura is not present in the submitted material.

Lymphovascular invasion: Focal, present (one vessel identified with intraluminal tumor cells in peri-tumoral tissue).
Perineural invasion: Not identified in the submitted material.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on 4-micron sections from formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded tissue):

  CK20: POSITIVE -- strong and diffuse cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells
  CDX2: POSITIVE -- moderate to strong nuclear staining, diffuse distribution (>90% of tumor cells)
  CK7: NEGATIVE -- no cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells (background stromal/inflammatory cells non-reactive)
  TTF-1 (SP141 clone): NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in neoplastic cells, confirming this is not a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma
  Napsin-A: NEGATIVE -- no cytoplasmic staining in neoplastic cells
  Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin-A: NEGATIVE
  p40: NEGATIVE

IHC INTERPRETATION: The immunophenotype (CK20+/CDX2+/CK7-/TTF-1-/Napsin-A-) is consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin. The morphologic and immunohistochemical profile is incompatible with a primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma.

MMR PROTEIN IHC (repeated on current specimen for completeness):
  MLH1: RETAINED (nuclear positivity in tumor cells)
  MSH2: RETAINED (nuclear positivity in tumor cells)
  MSH6: RETAINED (nuclear positivity in tumor cells)
  PMS2: RETAINED (nuclear positivity in tumor cells)
  INTERPRETATION: Mismatch repair proficient (pMMR), consistent with microsatellite stable (MSS) phenotype. No loss of MMR protein expression identified. Findings concordant with prior IHC on primary colonic biopsy.

TISSUE ALLOCATION:
  - Two additional unstained slides cut at 4 microns and retained for molecular testing
  - FFPE block and remaining core biopsy material submitted to molecular pathology laboratory for next-generation sequencing (comprehensive genomic profiling)

---

DIAGNOSIS:

RIGHT LOWER LOBE, LUNG -- CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

METASTATIC MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY.

  - Morphology and immunoprofile (CK20+, CDX2+, CK7-, TTF-1-, Napsin-A-) confirm colorectal origin; primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma excluded.
  - Mismatch repair proteins retained by IHC (pMMR/MSS), concordant with prior primary tumor testing.
  - Tissue allocated for comprehensive molecular re-profiling (see separate NGS report to follow).

---

Note: This case was reviewed and signed out by the attending surgical pathologist. Correlation with clinical history, prior pathologic material (SP-[accession number of original sigmoid biopsy]), and forthcoming molecular profiling results is advised for integrated clinical decision-making.

Electronic signature: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
